message.content:
Test file: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Implementation file: src/claim-office.ts
Failing test: "claim referencing item type not in policy throws"
Passing tests so far: 19

Make `runScenario` throw when a claim damage's `itemType` doesn't match any item in the referenced policy.

Per spec also: "if the damages array contains more entries of a given type than the policy actually covers (e.g. two sword damages but only one sword insured) → the CLI exits with a non-zero status code; the whole claim is rejected" — but ONE TEST AT A TIME. Don't implement the count-based rejection yet, just the "item type not in policy" case.

Minimal implementation: in processClaim, when processing damages, if no policy item has the same `type` as damage's `itemType`, throw an Error.

Make sure all existing tests still pass — they all use items that ARE in the policy.

Run `pnpm test` to verify all 20 pass.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, return after completion.

content.text: I'll analyze the current state and implement the minimal change to make the failing test pass.
Now I'll make the minimal change to `processClaim` to throw when a damage's `itemType` doesn't match any item in the policy.

content.text:
All 20 tests pass.

Green Phase Complete:
**Implementation**: Added a guard in `calculateDamagePayout` that throws an `Error` when the damage's `itemType` doesn't match any item in the referenced policy.
**Result**: All 20 tests now pass (18 still todo).
**Approach**: Minimal change in the single spot where damages get mapped to policy items via `items.find(...)` — when `find` returns `undefined`, we throw. No count-based rejection logic added (saved for a future test). All existing tests continue to pass because they use damages whose `itemType` matches an insured item.

Relevant files:
- /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-17_03-23-26_claim-office-example-mapping_v7-hybrid-green-refactor_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.ts
